calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2005 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-25-2005 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the Department of the Presiding Judge for Court Trial Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Trina Ayala.
Duncan MacDonald, Esq. for Plaintiff: Sword to Plowshares.
Plaintiff's witness: Chris Andoe, was sworn and direct examined.
No appearance by the Defendant: Lawrence L. Lynch.
Mr. MacDonald stated in open court, that he just found out last night the defendant pass away.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant as follows:
Restitution and possession of the premises located at 1029 Girard Road, #104B, San Francisco.
Plaintiff waived all back rent, damages, costs and fees.
Rental Agreement declared forfeited.
Judgment signed in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-21-2005 Continued To Jul-25-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-21-2005 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: DUCAN SCOTT MACDONALD FOR PLAINTIFF SWORDS TO PLOWSHARES
NO APPEARANCE BY DEFENDANT LAWRENCE L LYNCH

SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE OFF CALENDAR. JURY DEMAND STRICKEN. TO PROCEED AS COURT TRIAL.

JUDGE: OLLIE MARIE VICTOIRE; CLERK:BARBARA MORRIS; NOT REPORTED (218)

Notice Of Motion To Have Facts In Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Jun-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Duncan MacDonald, for Plaintiff/moving party
Lawrence Lynch, Defendant, appeared and left after he was told the matter was taken off calendar
Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Have Facts In Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted is ordered off calendar as the motion was not timely filed and served. See C.C.P. Section 1005(b). COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Have Facts In Requests For Admission Deemd Admitted
Off Calendar May-26-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Duncan MacDonald, appearing for plaintiff and moving party.
Lawrence Lynch, Defendant, appeared in Propria Persona (arrived late)

Ruling:
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Have Facts In Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted is ordered off calendar as the proof of service and service are inadequate. Personal service requires service upon a person. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Duncan MacDonald, Esq. of Chapman, Popik & White, 415-352-3000 for plaintiff and opposing party Swords to Plowshares.

RULING:
NO Other APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
DEFENDANT LAWRENCE LYNCH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled with 5 days leave to answer. Order signed. (301/DM/AC) Reporter: Rhonda Fung, CSR: 4819
